BLite.Server

Preview
🖥️Overview📦Installation🔌Protocol & API🎨Web Studio📡Client SDKs🔗Version Matrix

🔗 Version Matrix

BLite.Server wraps a specific version of BLite (the embedded engine). Use this table to ensure your server build and your embedded engine are compatible.

BLite.Server ↔ BLite Engine

BLite.ServerBLite EngineStatusRelease DateNotes
0.1.43.6.4Preview2026-03-13Initial public preview. gRPC, REST, Studio, multi-tenant, BLQL, Transactions, KV Store.

Client SDK Compatibility

All client SDKs target the gRPC protocol version defined by the Protobuf contracts in BLite.Proto. A protocol version bump happens only when breaking changes are made to the service definitions or wire format. Minor and patch releases of BLite.Server are always backward-compatible with the same major client SDK version.

BLite.ServerBLite.Client (.NET)@blite/client (npm)blite-client-java
0.1.x0.1.x0.1.x0.1.x

Runtime Requirements

ComponentRequirement
BLite.ServerSelf-contained binary (no .NET runtime required on host). Bundled .NET 10 runtime.
BLite.Client (.NET).NET 8+ or .NET 10. Supports ASP.NET Core dependency injection.
@blite/client (TypeScript)Node.js 18+. ESM and CommonJS builds included.
blite-client-javaJava 21+. Requires sealed interface pattern matching. Spring Boot 3.x optional.

Protocol Versioning

The BLite Server wire protocol follows this stability contract:

  • Major version (0 → 1): may include breaking changes to gRPC service definitions, REST path structure, or the BLQL filter serialization format.
  • Minor version (0.1 → 0.2): additive changes only — new RPC methods, new REST endpoints, new filter operators. Older clients continue to work.
  • Patch version (0.1.1 → 0.1.4): bug fixes and security patches. No protocol changes.
⚠ Preview period (0.x): Until the 1.0 release, even minor version bumps may include breaking changes. Pin your client and server versions to the same minor release during the preview period.
